body:
I'm Darius. If you're reading this, it means I'm dead.
My security system — Overwatch — has 14 days of autonomy.
After that, it sends this email to whoever has terminal access.
I created Overwatch for Nexus Corporation. I thought it was for
protecting critical infrastructure. I was wrong.
Nexus uses it to surveil every citizen. They sell the data to
the highest bidder. Governments, corporations, whoever pays.
The files they're trying to bury are on their servers.
SCAN <host> to find servers. CONNECT to enter.
Every server has ports. CRACK to brute force the password.
Start with: SCAN desk-11.corporate.com

Nexus has 3 server tiers. Corporate servers on the surface,
internal servers in the middle, and mainframes deep down.
Government servers are protected by military-grade encryption.
Use DECRYPT to decrypt keys. Each key requires
computing power. Upgrade CPU with UPGRADE.
I left weak passwords scattered around. Try LOGIN with:
  "admin123" on port 999
  "letmein" on port 25
  "root!" on port 21
Passwords are the weakest link in every system.

Nexus traces every connection. Normal ISPs log everything.
You need to use GSM burner phones for sensitive operations.
You'll find them on the black market (MARKET). Each phone gives
you an anonymous connection that halves the trace.
Use BOUNCE to hop between servers and cover your tracks.
More hops in the chain, the harder you are to find.
Buy one before hitting important servers.

sub: The Evidence I Was Looking For
body:
I hid data on government servers. Classified intel
that proves Nexus's involvement with the NSA.
Project Chimera, C4I Blueprint, NSA Backdoor Vault —
they're all there. Use INTEL to see what you've stolen,
then SELLINTEL to sell it on the black market.
Money is needed to upgrade your hardware.
The more power you have, the deeper you can go.

Nexus has a hidden mainframe. It contains the heart of Overwatch.
The source code, secret contracts, the client list.
Governments, dictatorships, corporations — everyone buys from Nexus.
They've buried evidence of murders, covered up disasters,
manipulated elections. All through Overwatch.
In the mainframe there is a switch. A single choice:
RELEASE EVERYTHING TO THE PUBLIC — every file, every transaction,
or DELETE EVERYTHING — bury the truth forever.
The choice is yours. I didn't have time to choose.

They found out I was investigating. On March 14th
they revoked my clearance. The next day
"car accident". Dead on impact.
Overwatch kept running without me.
For 14 days it gathered evidence. Then it found you.
You're the only one who knows. The only one who can do something.
The Nexus mainframe is on server "nexus-core.darknet".
It's protected by 5 firewalls. You'll need everything
you've learned. Good luck.
— Darius
